findings.title: Public exchange uses Curve with `min_dy = 0`, enabling flash-loan price manipulation and value extraction

best_agent_match.title: Public swap executes against Curve with zero slippage protection, enabling flash-loan value extraction

findings.title: Anyone can trigger cross-bank issuance and drain `from_bank` liquidity without authorization

best_agent_match.title: Hardcoded Curve pool address creates centralization risk

rejected_candidates.title: Swapped funds are sent to `to_bank` via raw ERC20 transfer with no deposit/accounting handshake
rejected_candidates.reason: Speculative. The available source only shows a raw token transfer into `to_bank`; there is no evidence that the destination bank requires an explicit deposit hook or that passive ERC20 receipt is insufficient for its accounting.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked return value from external token bank balance call
rejected_candidates.reason: Not a standalone vulnerability in this code. It relies on a malicious or broken `from_bank` dependency returning false data, which is outside the demonstrated trust model.

rejected_candidates.title: Constructor makes unsafe external calls without try-catch
rejected_candidates.reason: A constructor reverting on bad parameters or incompatible external contracts is expected deployment-time behavior, not a reportable protocol vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: No validation of exchange output amount before transfer
rejected_candidates.reason: Duplicate of the missing slippage protection issue. The lack of `min_dy` already captures the harmful acceptance of arbitrarily poor output.

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded Curve pool address creates centralization risk
rejected_candidates.reason: Operational rigidity / maintainability concern, but not a concrete exploitable vulnerability causing protocol-level harm on its own.

rejected_candidates.title: No event logging for doExchange execution
rejected_candidates.reason: Observability issue only; not a security finding.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential reentrancy vulnerability in safeApprove pattern
rejected_candidates.reason: Unsupported. The function has no sensitive intermediate state to exploit via reentrancy, and the report depends on a malicious Curve/token assumption not evidenced here.

rejected_candidates.title: Unused SafeMath library functions in main contract
rejected_candidates.reason: Code quality issue only; not a security vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: No deadline parameter in exchange function
rejected_candidates.reason: Not a distinct vulnerability here. The meaningful economic risk is already covered by the missing slippage protection, and the function is permissionless rather than user-order driven.
